specification.summary: Illustrative L-shaped mounting bracket with two circular holes.
specification.coordinate_system: relative units; largest dimension normalized to 1.0; origin at lower-plate base center; +X across width, +Y front-to-rear, +Z upward
overall_dimensions: overall_width: 0.65 relative; plausible range: 0.58-0.72; status: observed; confidence: medium; basis: visible silhouette
overall_height: 1.0 relative; plausible range: 1.0-1.0; status: observed; confidence: high; basis: normalization dimension
overall_depth: 0.8 relative; plausible range: 0.65-0.95; status: inferred; confidence: low; basis: foreshortened lower plate
specification.camera_estimate: Perspective view; azimuth about 35 degrees; elevation about 25 degrees; roll about 0 degrees; normal focal character; target near object center; medium confidence.
observed_geometry: Two perpendicular rectangular plate surfaces form the primary L silhouette.
One circular opening is visible on each plate.
inferred_geometry: The two plates likely share a constant wall thickness.
Both circular openings are likely through holes.

components.name: Bent bracket body
components.geometry: Two perpendicular rectangular plates of equal apparent thickness; front vertical face, lower top face, and right edge are visible.
components.cad_strategy: Sketch an L-shaped side profile and extrude it symmetrically across the object width.
dimensions: wall_thickness: 0.08 relative; plausible range: 0.06-0.10; status: observed; confidence: medium; basis: visible edge thickness
components.pose: Root component at the origin; upright vertical plate; mirror-symmetric across the central X plane.

features.description: Hole centered laterally in the vertical plate.
parameters: diameter: unknown; status: observed; confidence: low

features.description: Hole centered laterally near the lower plate's free end.
parameters: diameter: unknown; status: observed; confidence: low

assumptions: The two plates have equal thickness.
Both circular openings are through holes.
clarification_questions: What is the real overall height?
Are either of the holes countersunk or counterbored?
build_strategy: 1. Sketch the bracket_body L profile and extrude it symmetrically; risk: depth and wall thickness remain approximate.
2. Cut vertical_mounting_hole normal to the vertical plate; depends on bracket_body; risk: diameter is unknown.
3. Cut base_mounting_hole normal to the lower plate; depends on bracket_body; risk: position is affected by perspective.
evaluation_anchors: critical | front-right isometric | perpendicular plates preserve the dominant L silhouette
major | front-right isometric | one circular opening lies on each plate
